summary: Iteration 25 — Scanner + EA terminal 5-min filter + admin instant signal + scan token purchase flow. 25/25 backend pytest pass at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration25_scanner.py. Covered: (1) /mobile/trade-signals correctly drops signals older than 5 minutes (10-min-old signal excluded, 30-sec-old returned). (2) /admin/broker-connections/{lk}/signal/instant: 401 no auth, 403 mentor JWT, 404 unknown licence, 200 happy path inserts trade_signal with status set directly to executed/closed (no pending lifecycle), platform='manual', issued_by='server', issued_by_email=admin, instant=true, delivered_at+ack_at populated so /bridge/jobs skips it, result.mt_order_id + result.note persisted. (3) /mobile/scanner/execute-request: 403 wrong email, 404 unknown scan, 403 not-your-scan, 400 NEUTRAL scan rejected, 200 happy path sets execution_status='verifying' + execution_requested_at, idempotent on repeat (returns already_requested=true). (4) /mobile/scanner/purchase creates scan_purchases doc with status='pending', plan/price/scans correctly mapped (100→R350/100 scans, unlimited→R730/-1). (5) /admin/scan-purchases admin-only (403 for mentor), returns purchases sorted DESC, no _id leakage. (6) /admin/scan-purchases/{id}/approve: '100' plan increments users.scans_balance by 100, 'unlimited' plan sets users.scans_plan='unlimited', purchase marked status=approved with approved_by=admin email, re-approve returns 400. (7) /admin/scan-purchases/{id}/decline: 403 for mentor, marks status='declined' with provided decline_reason. (8) /admin/scans now returns execution_requested_at and execution_status fields. (9) Regression: /mobile/scanner/balance returns scans_balance/scans_plan/plans shape, /admin/users/{email}/scan-topup '100' and 'custom' both still work, /admin/broker-connections/{lk}/signal (queued push) still creates pending trade_signal without instant flag. (10) /mobile/scanner/upload happy path with a real generated candle-chart PNG → 200, direction in BUY/SELL/NEUTRAL, confidence 0-100, scans_balance debited 5→4, db.scans row inserted; 402 when scans_balance=0 + no unlimited plan.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is now 2693 lines — still ~3.8× the 700-line guideline. The scanner family (/mobile/scanner/upload, /balance, /execute-request, /purchase) and the admin scan-purchases CRUD are a clean candidate for routers/scanner.py + routers/admin_scans.py.

POST /api/admin/broker-connections/{license_key}/signal/instant does NOT verify symbol membership in pair_configs (per spec, intentional). However it also does not verify the licence is bound to a real broker_connection — admin could push a trade-signal terminal entry for a licence whose broker row was deleted. Consider at least a soft warning log so admin sees this.

POST /api/mobile/scanner/purchase accepts proof_data_url up to 4MB but only does .startswith('data:') validation — it does not enforce data:image/{png,jpeg,webp} like /scanner/upload does. A user could submit any base64 (PDF, text) as 'proof'. Tighten to startswith('data:image/').
POST /api/admin/scan-purchases/{id}/decline writes approved_at on a declined purchase — semantically wrong; consider renaming to decided_at or adding a separate declined_at field.

POST /api/mobile/scanner/upload returns scans_balance = max(0, old-1) computed in-process from the pre-update user doc rather than from the post-update doc. Under concurrent uploads the returned value can be stale. Use find_one_and_update to read the post-decrement value atomically.
/api/admin/scan-purchases has no pagination — limit(200) is hardcoded. Add a query-string `limit` like /admin/scans does.

/mobile/trade-signals 5-min cutoff is computed in Python from datetime.now() and compared as ISO string. This works because created_at is stored as ISO strings with timezone, but if a future migration ever stores naive datetimes or different ISO precision the string-comparison filter silently breaks. Consider storing created_at as a real BSON datetime and querying with $gte datetime.
test_credentials: Mentor test_it6_ui_1778753851@test.com / Passw0rd!, licence EAC-2F9D-E69F-6F75-CEB3 (unbound at teardown). Admin admin@ea-central.com / Admin@123. Auth token key: access_token.
seed_data_creation: At module setup: license bound to mentor email + device 'iter25-test-dev', broker_connections row upserted status=approved, pair_configs EURUSD BOTH 0.10 lot upserted, users.scans_balance reset to 5 and scans_plan unset, trade_signals/scans/scan_purchases for the licence/email cleared. At module teardown: trade_signals/scans/scan_purchases for the licence/email cleared again, original user scans state restored (snapshot taken before tests), broker_connections + pair_configs left in place (or deleted if they didn't exist pre-test), licence unbound (bound_to_email=null, bound_device_id=null) so next iteration can re-pair via /app.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 25 iteration-25 assertions live at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration25_scanner.py and pass in ~6s. /app/backend/server.py now exposes: /mobile/trade-signals (5-min rolling filter, last 20), /admin/broker-connections/{lk}/signal/instant (bypasses bridge queue, sets executed/closed/low_balance/failed directly with platform='manual' + instant=true + delivered_at+ack_at populated), /mobile/scanner/execute-request (records intent only — does NOT auto-create a trade_signal — admin must still push from /admin/scans), /mobile/scanner/purchase + /admin/scan-purchases CRUD (status: pending → approved/declined, approval mutates users.scans_balance or users.scans_plan). /admin/scans now returns execution_requested_at + execution_status. Scanner upload uses GPT-5.2 vision via emergentintegrations (LlmChat .with_model('openai','gpt-5.2'), ImageContent). DB collections in use: trade_signals, scans, scan_purchases, broker_connections, pair_configs, license_keys, users. Licence EAC-2F9D-E69F-6F75-CEB3 is unbound at teardown.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found. 25/25 backend assertions pass including the live GPT-5.2 vision call on a generated 600×360 candle-chart PNG. All four new feature flows (terminal 5-min filter, admin instant signal, scanner execute-request, scan token purchase with admin approve/decline) behave per spec and the three regression endpoints (scanner balance, admin scan-topup, admin queued signal) are unaffected.
